problem.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock

sources.notes: Two-player deletion/locking game on an array; maximize the final stock beauty.
Official editorial binary-searches the target beauty g and counts, capped at 3, how many opposite-side indices can form a pair with each index.

annotation.summary: Compute the game value where one player tries to guarantee a pair with value difference at least g and the other deletes one index first.
annotation.constraints: n is large and values are up to 1e9, so the target beauty is binary-searched and each check must be linear.
annotation.core_idea: For a target g, define f_g(i) as the number of other indices j on the appropriate side with value difference at least g, capped at 3. If after Hao removes one index all f_g(i)<=1, Alex cannot force a pair; otherwise Alex can. During the check, only the three smallest previous values and three largest suffix values matter to decide whether each f_g(i) reaches 2 or more. Then test whether some first removal covers all vertices with degree 2 or more in this threshold graph.
annotation.complexity: O(n log V) per test case.

solution_variants.name: threshold-game-binary-search
solution_variants.summary: Binary search g; for each g, compute capped threshold-pair counts and test whether the first player can still force a valid pair after one deletion.

tags.evidence: The game value is found by binary searching the target beauty g.
The feasibility check explicitly analyzes Hao's first deletion and Alex's ability to force a remaining pair.
The check only distinguishes whether each index has 0, 1, 2, or more than 2 qualifying partners.
